name: Event-sourced Peer (CRDT / Scuttlebutt)
discovery_mechanism: Gossip from known peers; no central directory
strengths: Works offline. Two halves of a partition keep functioning and reconcile when they meet. No server to take down or compromise. Identity is cryptographically self-validating.
tradeoffs: Logs grow forever — needs compaction. Discovery is hard without central directories. Per-recipient encryption defeats cache sharing. Eventual consistency isn't always what users expect ('why didn't my message show up yet?').
real_world_examples: Scuttlebutt (SSB). Briar. Yjs/Automerge for collaborative editing. Matrix federation. git itself.
